lede: A look at February 24 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Wilhelm Grimm and Ibn Battuta, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: United Airlines Flight 811
events.desc: United Airlines Flight 811, bound for New Zealand from Honolulu, rips open during flight, blowing nine passengers out of the business-class section.

events.title: Yuba County Five
events.desc: The Yuba County Five disappear in California. Four of their bodies are found four months later.

events.title: Vietnam War
events.desc: Vietnam War: The Tet Offensive is halted; South Vietnamese forces led by Ngo Quang Truong recapture the citadel of Hué.

events.title: Egypt
events.desc: Egyptian Premier Ahmad Mahir Pasha is killed in Parliament after reading a decree.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: First large-scale protest march resulting in clashes with the Axis occupation forces and collaborationist police in Athens against rumours of forced mobilization of Greek workers for work in Germany.

events.title: World War I
events.desc: World War I: The U.S. ambassador Walter Hines Page to the United Kingdom is given the Zimmermann Telegram, in which Germany pledges to ensure the return of New Mexico, Texas, and Arizona to Mexico if Mexico declares war on the United States.

events.title: Andrew Johnson
events.desc: Andrew Johnson becomes the first President of the United States to be impeached by the United States House of Representatives. He is later acquitted in the Senate.

events.title: Treaty of Yandabo
events.desc: The signing of the Treaty of Yandabo marks the end of the First Anglo-Burmese War.

events.title: Theatre Royal, Drury Lane
events.desc: London's Drury Lane Theatre burns to the ground, leaving its owner, Irish writer and politician Richard Brinsley Sheridan, destitute.

events.title: Marbury v. Madison
events.desc: In Marbury v. Madison, the Supreme Court of the United States establishes the principle of judicial review.

births.name: Leo von Caprivi
births.line: German general and politician, Chancellor of Germany

births.name: Steve Jobs
births.line: American businessman, co-founded Apple Computer and Pixar

deaths.text: Sandro Pertini, Italian journalist and politician, 7th President of Italy
Nikolai Bulganin, Russian marshal and politician, 6th Premier of the Soviet Union
Hjalmar Branting, Swedish journalist and politician, 16th Prime Minister of Sweden, Nobel Prize laureate
François, Duke of Guise, Duke of Guise

observances.text: Christian feast day: Blessed Ascensión Nicol y Goñi
Christian feast day: Blessed Josefa Naval Girbés
Christian feast day: Lindel Tsen and Paul Sasaki (Anglican Church of Canada)
